sourceNote: 翻译原文和参考译文已根据公开真题页面核验。

items.sourceText:
We tend to think that friends and family members are our biggest sources of connection, laughter, and warmth. While that may well be true, researchers have also recently found that interacting with strangers actually brings a boost in mood and feelings of belonging that we didn't expect.
 In one series of studies, researchers instructed Chicago-area commuters using public transportation to strike up a conversation with someone near them. On average, participants who followed this instruction felt better than those who had been told to stand or sit in silence. The researchers also argued that when we shy away from casual interactions with strangers, it is often due to a misplaced anxiety that they might not want to talk to us. Much of the time, however, this belief is false. As it turns out, many people are actually perfectly willing to talk—and may even be flattered to receive your attention.
items.referenceTranslation: 我们往往认为，朋友和家人是人际关系、快乐与温暖最重要的源泉。这话固然不假，但研究人员最近还发现：与陌生人交流，其实能带来意想不到的心情提升和归属感。在一系列研究中，研究者要求芝加哥乘坐公共交通工具的通勤者与身旁的人交谈。结果显示，平均而言，遵照指示的参与者比那些被要求保持沉默的人感觉更佳。研究者还指出，我们之所以回避与陌生人随意交流，往往源于一种不必要的忧虑——担心对方不愿搭理自己。然而多数时候，这种想法并不正确。事实上，很多人其实非常乐意交谈，甚至因受到你的关注而感到荣幸。
